intro: Coleosanthus amplexicaulis, also known as the South American shrub or Sida, is a flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, wounds, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Coleosanthus amplexicaulis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health conditions.
